messages.content:
working solo so far, looking to team up if I make it to stage 2. also: anyone know if team composition can change between stages? not seeing it in the rules.

quick sketch of where I am:

  • on the math side, derived and wrote up a structural theorem about a family of equation pairs that admit algorithmic implication checks. preprint on Zenodo, Lean 4 formalization in a public repo.
  • on the tooling side, built out a local stack for the full magma-implication task: exhaustive CE search, full-graph rule validation (sound rules verified 0-wrong across all 4694×4694 pairs), per-failure diagnostics, stratified eval subsets, reproducible build pipeline. reusable beyond this specific competition.
  • I've also accumulated a fairly large corpus of model-response data from stage 1 probing: tens of thousands of per-problem results across five frontier models (gpt-oss-120b, gpt-oss-20b, gemma-4-31b, llama-3.3-70b, deepseek-v3.2), every response auto-classified into a fine-grained failure-mode taxonomy at ~99% agreement with hand labels. queryable by what kind of wrong, not just wrong.

looking for: equational logic / universal algebra, Lean, or strong math + curiosity. I collaborate heavily with AI on the engineering side, heads up if that's a dealbreaker.

DM if interested.
messages.subject: looking for stage 2 collaborators

messages.content: @Adam McKenna Yes, the team composition can change. You need to register again and create a new team for stage 2. Thank you!
messages.subject: looking for stage 2 collaborators
